get.description: Retrieve all action schedules in the system

parameters.description:
Custom HTTP header name to be used by Suite API clients to acknowledge the fact
that the client intends to use a Suite API under the "/internal" endpoint even
though the backwards compatibility for these APIs is not guaranteed.
parameters.name: X-Ops-API-use-unsupported

application/xml.example:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<ops:action-automation-schedules xmlns:ops="http://webservice.vmware.com/vRealizeOpsMgr/1.0/" xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
    <ops:actionSchedules>
        <ops:actionSchedule id="39acaf95-84fc-420f-a7f0-0f59c6bdd7c0">
            <ops:name>Rem schedule</ops:name>
            <ops:description>Action schedule</ops:description>
            <ops:enabled>true</ops:enabled>
            <ops:actionDetails xsi:type="ops:schedule-additional-action-details">
                <ops:actionKey>
                    <ops:key>Power Off VM</ops:key>
                    <ops:adapterKindKey>VMWARE</ops:adapterKindKey>
                </ops:actionKey>
            </ops:actionDetails>
            <ops:actionScope xsi:type="ops:additional-action-schedule-scope">
                <ops:resourceIds>
                    <ops:resourceId>46c17c1b-c144-4451-816e-dd13301be8e6</ops:resourceId>
                </ops:resourceIds>
                <ops:contextResourceKindKey>
                    <ops:resourceKind>HostSystem</ops:resourceKind>
                    <ops:adapterKind>VMWARE</ops:adapterKind>
                </ops:contextResourceKindKey>
                <ops:filters>
                    <ops:filter>
                        <ops:scopeFilters>
                            <ops:scopeFilter xsi:type="ops:metric-filter">
                                <ops:key>summary|total_memory_usage</ops:key>
                                <ops:numericOperator>LESS_THAN</ops:numericOperator>
                                <ops:numericValue>25.5</ops:numericValue>
                            </ops:scopeFilter>
                        </ops:scopeFilters>
                    </ops:filter>
                </ops:filters>
            </ops:actionScope>
            <ops:scheduleConfig>
                <ops:timeZone>Asia/Seoul</ops:timeZone>
                <ops:startDate>2021-06-25T10:15</ops:startDate>
                <ops:scheduleType xsi:type="ops:daily-schedule">
                    <ops:recurrenceDayCount>5</ops:recurrenceDayCount>
                </ops:scheduleType>
                <ops:expirationDate>2021-06-26</ops:expirationDate>
                <ops:pluginConfigs>
                    <ops:pluginConfig xsi:type="ops:email-plugin-config">
                        <ops:id>pluginId</ops:id>
                        <ops:receiverEmail>example_user@gmail.com</ops:receiverEmail>
                    </ops:pluginConfig>
                </ops:pluginConfigs>
            </ops:scheduleConfig>
            <ops:modifiedByUser>admin</ops:modifiedByUser>
            <ops:modificationTime>2021-06-17T10:15:30</ops:modificationTime>
            <ops:lastRunStartTime>2021-06-18T08:00</ops:lastRunStartTime>
            <ops:nextRunStartTime>2021-06-19T08:00</ops:nextRunStartTime>
        </ops:actionSchedule>
    </ops:actionSchedules>
</ops:action-automation-schedules>

get.description: Get the summary of all cost drivers for the selected cloud type. Cost Drivers are the expense types used by the VMware Cloud Foundation Operations to calculate the cost of your private cloud. The total private cloud cost is the sum of cost drivers. Changes that are made to Cost Drivers are reflected only after the next run of the cost engine. You can trigger a manual cost calculation and check its status in the Cost Calculation Status tab.

post.description:
Imports integrations in ZIP format.
NOTE: In the response: resourceKey of integrations will include only unique identifiers; id of integration always refers to existing entity, whereas other fields refer to external data; for failed integrations id will be null in case if reason is ADAPTER_KIND_NOT_FOUND; 'fields' in credential instances will not be returned.
NOTE: Upon completion of the import process, it is recommended to check integration accounts. Some collectors or collector groups may have become unbound from the accounts they serve and should be reconnected. Additionally, you will need to re-accept all untrusted certificates manually. Finally ensure that all your adapter instances are started as some of these may not have been restarted automatically.

event.description: Class to represent external events.  Different event types have different required fields.

cancelTimeUTC.description:
Indicates that an event has been cancelled. For an externally managed event a new event with
the same message value must be sent with a cancelTime in order to cancel the
event.

eventType.description:
Represents the type of an Event.
All values are valid for the field eventType in the Event object.
When used in the creation of a SymptomDefinition the following restrictions are
applicable for the Conditions that are defined as part of a Symptom Definition.

htType.description: The type of Metric Event or Metric Event Condition

key.description: The StatKey for the condition of the HardThreshold event

keyIndicator.description:
Whether the HardThreshold event is a key performance indicator
True indicates it is a KPI. False otherwise.

managedExternally.description:
If set to true, this event shall persist across collection cycles until another event is pushed
to change the status.
If set to false, this event will be cancelled during the next collection cycle if it is not pushed again

message.description: Event message, the message identifies the event in the system.

resourceId.description: The id of the resource for the event

startTimeUTC.description:
The start time for the event in UTC.
This time must be after >= 946684800000L, or January 1, 2000. Default is 946684800000L.
Value is ignored for Change events.

value.description: The current value (not the expected value) of the metric for the HardThreshold event

Represents the type of an Event.
All values are valid for the field eventType in the Event object.
When used in the creation of a SymptomDefinition the following restrictions are
applicable for the Conditions that are defined as part of a Symptom Definition.

message.description:
The message is the string that the event's message must
either match exactly if operator is CompareOperator.EQ or
be a substring if operator is CompareOperator.CONTAINS
NOTE: Also in this case the message must be 3-128 characters long and the
symptom from the event must be IN the message
